channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Well, that's unexpected: Samsung will team up with its fiercest Chinese rival to produce next gen NAND flash
item.description: Samsung has signed an agreement with China’s YMTC to use its bonding technology in the production of 400-layer NAND flash memory.
item.dc:creator: waynewilliams@onmail.com (Wayne Williams), TechRadar

item.title: Microsoft Unveils Preview of AI-Powered Chat App Template
item.description: Microsoft has announced the preview release of a .NET web app template, designed to simplify the development of AI-powered chat applications.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Trump hosts crypto leaders at White House summit
item.description: The cryptocurrency industry flocked to Washington on Friday for the White House’s first-ever crypto summit, where President Trump and his Cabinet made clear the “war on crypto” is over in Washington.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: Google’s ‘quiet’ decision to remove cultural events from its Calendar app sent a loud message
item.description: Starting today, thousands of changemakers and leaders will descend upon Austin for one of the biggest festivals and conferences of the year: South by Southwest (SXSW).
item.dc:creator: Jennifer Risi, Fast Company

item.title: We asked the world’s most creative people how they’re using AI. This is what they said
item.description: How are the world’s most creative people using AI to drive their work forward? This was the question at the heart of an in-depth survey Fast Company recently conducted in partnership with Whalar Group, a global creator company.
item.dc:creator: Brendan Vaughan, Fast Company

item.title: Remembering how 18F modernized government technology
item.description: Healthcare.gov, the government health insurance marketplace website, launched in October 2013 only to buckle under the weight of just 2,000 simultaneous users.
item.dc:creator: The Conversation, Fast Company

item.title: Disco Elysium successor XXX Nightshift nets some serious musical talent
item.description: Disco Elysium successor XXX Nightshift will feature a soundtrack led by songwriter and music producer John Cunningham.
item.dc:creator: dash.wood@futurenet.com (Dashiell Wood), TechRadar

item.title: How this sex-forward gay cruising site finally launched an Apple-approved iOS app
item.description: As an app designed to facilitate gay hookups, popular site Sniffies has had a limitation since it launched in 2018—it was accessible only via web browser. Until now. On Monday, the map-based cruising site debuted its Apple-approved iOS app.
item.dc:creator: Henry Chandonnet, Fast Company

item.title: Trump signs executive order establishing bitcoin reserve
item.description: President Donald Trump signed an executive order Thursday establishing a government reserve of bitcoin, a key marker in the cryptocurrency's journey towards possible mainstream acceptance. Under Trump's new order, the U.S.
item.dc:creator: ALAN SUDERMAN, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: TSMC’s $100 billion U.S. commitment could calm Taiwan tensions
item.description: Welcome to AI Decoded, Fast Company’s weekly newsletter that breaks down the most important news in the world of AI. You can sign up to receive this newsletter every week here. TSMC’s new $100 billion U.S.
item.dc:creator: Mark Sullivan, Fast Company

item.title: Taiwan says chipmaker’s move to invest $100 billion in the US wasn’t because of US pressure
item.description: TAIPEI, Taiwan (AP) — Taiwan’s president and top chipmaker TSMC held a joint news conference Thursday to defend the company’s decision to invest $100 billion in the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, The Mercury News

item.title: AI chatbots infected with Russian disinformation: Study
item.description: The world’s most popular artificial intelligence (AI) chatbots are infected with Russian disinformation, according to a study published Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, The Hill

item.title: Monarch butterflies wintering in Mexico rebound this year
item.description: The number of monarch butterflies wintering in the mountains west of Mexico City, rebounded this year, doubling the area they covered in 2024 despite the stresses of climate change.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Apple appeals UK encryption backdoor demand
item.description: Apple has publicly been pushing back against a UK order that demands it create encryption backdoors, and it has now formally appealed that order to the UK Investigatory Powers Tribunal.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Review: Zencoder has a vision for AI coding
item.description: Zencoder is an AI coding assistant with interfaces to Visual Studio Code and JetBrains IDEs. It is designed to help developers ship products faster, much like GitHub Copilot, Amazon Q Developer, and Tabnine.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: How to boost Python program performance with Zig
item.description: Python might not be the fastest of languages, but it has many advantages. Users enjoy Python’s convenience and development speed, as well as its broad ecosystem of libraries. Something else people appreciate about Python is how it bridges language ecosystems.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Georgia lawmakers push to ban automated speed cameras near schools
item.description: DECATUR, Ga. (AP) — Outside Beacon Hill Middle School in the Atlanta suburb of Decatur, like along hundreds of roadsides across Georgia, the unblinking eye of a camera tickets drivers who speed through a school zone.
item.dc:creator: JEFF AMY, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: 2025 global network outage report and internet health check
item.description: The reliability of services delivered by ISPs, cloud providers and conferencing services is critical for enterprise organizations.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Cisco: Latest news and insights
item.description: Cisco is the dominant vendor in enterprise networking, and under CEO Chuck Robbins, it continues to shake things up. Its most recent mega-acquisition is its $28 billion purchase of Splunk, a bold move that adds revenue opportunities in observability, data analytics, AIOps, and genAI.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Tariffs an 'expensive game to play': Powerteam International CEO
item.description: Tariffs are going to be an "expensive game to play," Bill Walsh, CEO and founder of Powerteam International, said.
item.dc:creator: Connell McShane, NewsNation

item.title: The 2025 Terminator? Lab-grown muscle brings biohybrid robot hand to life
item.description: Kurt “CyberGuy" Knutsson talks about how lab-grown muscles power a biohybrid hand for scalable prosthetics and research.
item.dc:creator: Fox News
